title: BCDR: Publish DEH API on Microsoft Learn - Purview Data Governance
summary: This user story involves publishing the Data Estate Health (DEH) API documentation on Microsoft Learn under Purview Data Governance as part of Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCDR). It requires authoring a TypeSpec file, submitting it to the Azure SDK GitHub repository, and ensuring the API reference is live, accurate, and follows Microsoft standards.

risks.description: Inaccurate or incomplete API documentation could mislead customers and internal teams, impacting usability and support.
Delays in coordinating with the documentation team may postpone the publication on Microsoft Learn, affecting BCDR readiness.
Complexity in authoring the TypeSpec file and validating the OpenAPI spec may require specialized knowledge and cause delays.

action_items: Author the TypeSpec (TSP) definition for the DEH API.
Submit a pull request with the TSP file to the public Azure SDK GitHub repository (azure-rest-api-specs).
Validate that the generated OpenAPI specification from the TSP file is accurate and complete.
Coordinate with the documentation team to publish and link the API reference on the Microsoft Learn Purview Data Governance page.
Verify that the live API details, including version, endpoints, schemas, and error codes, appear correctly and meet Microsoft Learn and Azure SDK standards.

title: Replicate Billing Processing Units (PUs) per Customer from Log Analytics to Kusto
summary: This user story involves replicating per-customer Billing Processing Units (PUs) data from Log Analytics to a new Kusto table. The goal is to capture detailed billing PU metrics for three billing scopes (DEH, DQ, BYOC) including domain-split data, ensuring data consistency and retention of existing Log Analytics logging. The implementation includes adding a Kusto ingestion client, defining a suitable schema, and writing billing PU records after successful billing submissions.

risks.description: Potential data inconsistency between Log Analytics and Kusto if ingestion or schema mapping is incorrect.
Integration complexity with existing billing pipeline and ensuring no disruption to current Log Analytics logging.
Dependency on accurate and timely billing event processing to avoid missing or delayed data in Kusto.

action_items: Review existing MetersToBillingJobStage code to identify or reuse Kusto ingestion client.
Define and document the Kusto table schema capturing all required fields (AccountId, TenantId, DMSScope, ProcessingUnits, etc.).
Implement Kusto ingestion logic to write billing PU records after successful CBS billing submission.
Include both original and domain-enriched billing events in the Kusto ingestion.
Develop unit tests covering the new Kusto ingestion path to ensure data correctness.
Validate that Kusto data matches Log Analytics data for all three billing scopes.
Ensure existing Log Analytics logging remains unchanged throughout the implementation.

title: Redis Infra Onboarding for Reporting APIs
summary: This user story involves onboarding Redis infrastructure for Reporting APIs using Azure managed Redis (enterprise) to support Purview Data Governance's Data Estate Health area.

risks.description: Lack of detailed acceptance criteria may lead to unclear requirements and scope creep.
Dependency on Azure managed Redis service availability and configuration correctness.
Potential integration issues with existing Reporting APIs if Redis setup is misconfigured.

action_items: Define clear acceptance criteria for Redis onboarding.
Coordinate with Azure infrastructure team to provision and configure Azure managed Redis (enterprise).
Validate integration of Redis with Reporting APIs through testing.
Document the Redis infrastructure setup and usage guidelines for the team.
